title: Cyberattack on US Banking System
description: A coordinated cyberattack has knocked out online banking for an estimated 40 million Americans. ATMs are offline in 12 states. The FBI suspects a foreign state actor. Markets are down 3.2% and your press secretary is getting destroyed at the podium.

choices.text: Publicly blame the foreign power and issue immediate sanctions
choices.outcome: Your decisive response rallied the country behind you, but economists warn the sanctions could trigger retaliatory trade measures that hurt American consumers.

choices.text: Quietly investigate before making any public accusation
choices.outcome: Intelligence agencies confirmed the culprit within three weeks, but the media spent that time hammering you for weakness. Protests erupted near the White House.

choices.text: Deploy emergency CISA cyber defense teams and restore systems first
choices.outcome: A rapid CISA response contained the damage within 72 hours. The public rewarded competence over aggression. Approval ticks up modestly.

choices.text: Use the crisis to push a sweeping Cybersecurity Resilience Act
choices.outcome: Congress rallied behind the bill in a rare show of unity. Expensive, but the legislation sets a generational precedent for digital infrastructure protection.

title: Stock Market Drops 9% in a Single Day
description: A combination of inflation fears, weak jobs numbers, and a surprise Fed rate hike triggered the worst single-day selloff since 2020. Retirement accounts are bleeding. Wall Street wants intervention. Main Street wants someone to blame.

choices.text: Coordinate emergency response with the Fed and announce a stimulus package
choices.outcome: Markets stabilized after the joint announcement. The stimulus costs are real but the bleeding stopped. Wall Street breathes again.

delayed_effects.headline: Federal stimulus proving more inflationary than models predicted

choices.text: Let markets self-correct. No government intervention.
choices.outcome: Markets dropped another 4% the following week. The ideological consistency played well with fiscal hawks but devastated your approval among working-class voters.

delayed_effects.headline: Job losses mount as market correction ripples through employment

choices.text: Cut corporate taxes to restore business confidence
choices.outcome: Business confidence rebounded within a month. Progressive groups erupted. The base is fractured and your party is fighting itself in the press.

choices.text: Launch a major public works and infrastructure jobs program
choices.outcome: Job creation headlines dominated for weeks. Debt hawks in both parties are furious, but voters in swing states love the tangible results.

title: Record Border Crossings Trigger Political Firestorm
description: Monthly border crossing figures just hit an all-time high. Governors of four border states are demanding immediate federal action. The opposition is holding press conferences hourly. International media has descended on El Paso.

choices.text: Deploy National Guard units to supplement Border Patrol
choices.outcome: Crossings dropped within weeks. Civil liberties groups condemned the militarization. Your base is split and you're defending the decision in every interview.

delayed_effects.headline: Progressive base frustrated by compromise — feels administration gave away too much

choices.text: Fast-track asylum processing reform to clear the backlog humanely
choices.outcome: A measured approach praised internationally but slow to take visible effect. Critics call it doing nothing. Supporters call it doing the right thing.

choices.text: Negotiate root cause agreements with Mexico, Guatemala, and Honduras
choices.outcome: Diplomatic progress is real but invisible. The numbers won't move for 6 months. Critics call it weakness. Analysts call it the only durable solution.

delayed_effects.headline: International human rights groups condemn border enforcement practices

choices.text: Propose a comprehensive bipartisan immigration overhaul bill
choices.outcome: The bill collapsed in committee along party lines. You look principled. You got nothing done. The opposition immediately ran ads against you.

title: Category 5 Hurricane Devastates the Gulf Coast
description: Hurricane Vera made landfall near New Orleans with 185mph winds. 3.2 million without power. 450,000 displaced. FEMA is overwhelmed. The world is watching how you respond, and Katrina comparisons are already trending.

choices.text: Declare federal emergency and deploy full military logistics support
choices.outcome: A swift, coordinated federal response. The Katrina comparisons died quickly. Approval surges and bipartisan praise follows. The price tag will be debated for months.

choices.text: Defer primary response to state governors and offer federal backup
choices.outcome: Louisiana's governor was overwhelmed within 48 hours. The optics were catastrophic. Congress held emergency hearings. The Katrina comparisons never stopped.

delayed_effects.headline: FEMA disaster relief costs exceed initial estimates by $180 billion

choices.text: Personally visit the disaster zone in the first 24 hours
choices.outcome: Your visibility and hands-on presence reassured a shaken public. Critics called it performative. Polling disagreed strongly with critics.

choices.text: Fast-track federal housing vouchers and long-term rebuild funding
choices.outcome: Survivors got real help fast. The $2.5T price tag dominated congressional debate for the rest of the year. Your party held firm. Barely.

title: NATO Ally Under Military Attack
description: A key NATO ally is under sustained attack from a neighboring authoritarian state. The UN Security Council is deadlocked. Your Defense Secretary wants to act immediately. Your Treasury Secretary is warning of economic blowback. The public is war-weary.

choices.text: Send military equipment and intelligence support only
choices.outcome: A measured commitment. Allies are grateful. Isolationists in both parties are loudly frustrated. The conflict continues without US casualties.

choices.text: Deploy US troops under the NATO collective defense umbrella
choices.outcome: Anti-war protests erupted in 22 cities. Congress held emergency sessions. Allies rallied. The conflict entered a new and expensive phase.

choices.text: Lead aggressive international diplomatic coalition to force ceasefire
choices.outcome: Talks stalled for five weeks while the attack continued. You're being accused of appeasement at home and praised for restraint abroad.

choices.text: Impose crippling multilateral economic sanctions on the aggressor
choices.outcome: The aggressor's economy contracted 8% within a quarter. The war dragged on. The sanctions coalition held. Slow but measurable pressure.

title: Classified Documents Found at Advisor's Home
description: The FBI confirms classified materials were discovered at the private residence of your National Security Advisor. The opposition is demanding a special counsel within hours. Your press secretary is getting destroyed at the podium and three senators from your own party have gone silent.

choices.text: Fire the advisor immediately and announce full cooperation with FBI
choices.outcome: Swift accountability limited the damage. The story died within eight days. You looked decisive. The advisor's departure created a security gap that took months to fill.

choices.text: Defend the advisor publicly and dismiss it as a political hit job
choices.outcome: The base loved the fight. Independent voters broke away hard. The story dominated the news cycle for six straight weeks. Three senators announced investigations.

choices.text: Stay silent and let your legal team handle it privately
choices.outcome: Silence read as guilt. The media filled the vacuum with speculation. Approval bled two points per week. Your communications director threatened to quit.

choices.text: Call for an independent DOJ investigation and release what you can publicly
choices.outcome: The transparency play worked. Approval ticked up four points in a week. The investigation will last six months and create uncertainty, but you own the narrative.

title: FBI Disrupts Major Domestic Terror Plot
description: The FBI has arrested 8 individuals in a foiled attack on the Capitol building. The plot was sophisticated and foreign-linked. You have 24 hours before the story breaks publicly. How you frame this shapes the narrative entirely.

choices.text: Hold a joint press conference with FBI and DHS — lead with competence
choices.outcome: The competence narrative stuck. You're credited with keeping the country safe. Your approval gets its biggest single-month bump of the year.

choices.text: Use it to push emergency surveillance expansion powers
choices.outcome: Security hawks loved it. Civil liberties groups launched a massive campaign against you. The ACLU announced a legal challenge within 48 hours.

choices.text: Quietly announce the arrests and avoid politicizing the event
choices.outcome: A restrained, professional response. The press praised the tone. The opposition found nothing to attack. A small but clean win.

choices.text: Blame the opposition's rhetoric for radicalizing the suspects
choices.outcome: The accusation backfired spectacularly. Moderate voters were disgusted. Congress ground to a halt. Your Chief of Staff called it the worst political decision of the term.

title: New Viral Outbreak Detected in Three States
description: The CDC has flagged a novel respiratory virus spreading rapidly in Florida, Texas, and California. Early models suggest it could reach pandemic scale. The public is skittish after COVID. Your health advisors are divided on the severity. Acting too early or too late both carry enormous political risk.

choices.text: Declare a public health emergency and activate the pandemic response playbook
choices.outcome: You moved early. The outbreak was contained within two months. Economists complained about the disruption. The death toll was a fraction of COVID-19. History will be kind.

delayed_effects.headline: Early restrictions take toll as businesses report sharp revenue declines

choices.text: Issue a calm statement and monitor — avoid causing public panic
choices.outcome: The virus spread faster than monitoring could track. Within six weeks, three more states reported outbreaks. The 'stay calm' messaging became a political liability.

delayed_effects.headline: Critics say delayed response cost critical weeks as cases accelerate
Economic damage deepens as pandemic mitigation proves harder without early containment

choices.text: Fast-track emergency FDA authorization for a vaccine program
choices.outcome: The vaccine program created confidence and moved markets. A portion of the public refused the vaccine on principle. A vocal minority created a significant headache.

choices.text: Leave it to states — this is a state-level public health matter
choices.outcome: State responses were wildly inconsistent. The outbreak exploded. Congressional Democrats launched an investigation. The comparison to COVID became unavoidable.

title: Global Oil Prices Spike 40% After OPEC+ Cuts
description: OPEC+ announced surprise production cuts sending crude oil to $140/barrel. Gas prices at the pump are up $.90 overnight. Inflation that was cooling is now re-accelerating. Every economic advisor you have is giving you different advice.

choices.text: Release emergency reserves from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve
choices.outcome: Prices dropped back within three weeks. Security advisors flagged the reduced reserves as a vulnerability. A short-term political win with a long-term cost.

choices.text: Accelerate domestic drilling permits and fast-track LNG export terminals
choices.outcome: Energy industry praised the move. Climate groups went ballistic. The drilling won't affect prices for 18 months, but the optics were immediate.

choices.text: Negotiate directly with Saudi Arabia and UAE to increase production
choices.outcome: Quiet diplomacy worked. Production increased two months later. The press gave you no credit. The gas prices falling gave you plenty.

choices.text: Use the crisis to push a major clean energy transition bill
choices.outcome: The policy was right. The timing was politically disastrous. Voters wanted lower gas prices now, not renewable energy in five years. Midterm polling shifted hard.

title: Government Shutdown Looming in 72 Hours
description: Congress has failed to pass a continuing resolution. The Speaker is demanding major policy concessions your party finds unacceptable. 800,000 federal workers will miss paychecks Friday. Markets are nervous. You have 72 hours.

choices.text: Negotiate directly with the Speaker and accept a modified compromise
choices.outcome: A clean deal with real concessions. Both bases complained. The government stayed open. Moderate voters rewarded you in next month's polls.

choices.text: Hold firm — no concessions. Let the shutdown happen and blame Congress.
choices.outcome: The shutdown lasted 19 days. Federal workers were furious. Markets dipped 2%. The blame game played out exactly as you expected and moved no votes.

choices.text: Issue an emergency executive order to keep critical agencies funded
choices.outcome: Constitutional lawyers immediately challenged the order. Congress was furious at the end-run. The government stayed open. The legal fight lasted months.

choices.text: Take the debate public — hold prime time address to pressure Congress
choices.outcome: The address moved public opinion but not enough votes. A deal came together three days after the shutdown began. You got partial credit for the resolution.

title: National Protests Erupt After Controversial Police Shooting
description: A viral video of a police shooting has ignited protests in 40 cities. Two nights of property damage in Atlanta and Portland. Your Justice Department is monitoring. The political pressure is coming from every direction simultaneously.

choices.text: Meet with protest leaders and announce a DOJ civil rights investigation
choices.outcome: The gesture de-escalated most cities within a week. A vocal minority called it political theater. The DOJ investigation became a lightning rod for six months.

choices.text: Invoke the Insurrection Act and deploy National Guard to affected cities
choices.outcome: The crackdown intensified protests. Legal challenges flooded in. Governors of three states refused to comply. The images went global and damaged international standing.

choices.text: Push a comprehensive police reform and accountability bill through Congress
choices.outcome: The bill became a rallying point for reform supporters. Police unions launched a massive opposition campaign. The bill passed narrowly after six weeks of brutal negotiation.

choices.text: Issue a statement supporting law enforcement and call for patience
choices.outcome: The statement infuriated your base. Moderate conservatives appreciated the tone. Protests continued for two more weeks before burning out naturally.

title: Supreme Court Justice Announces Sudden Retirement
description: A sitting Justice has announced retirement effective in 60 days, giving you a Supreme Court nomination. This will be the most consequential appointment of your presidency. The Senate battle will consume everything for months.

choices.text: Nominate a moderate judge with a bipartisan record to unify the country
choices.outcome: The nomination sailed through with unexpected bipartisan support. Your base was furious about the compromise. The country got a confirmed Justice in record time.

delayed_effects.headline: Swing-state Democrats distance themselves ahead of confirmation fight

choices.text: Nominate a strong ideological ally to energize your base
choices.outcome: Your base erupted in celebration. The confirmation fight became the most brutal in Senate history. The nominee was confirmed by two votes after three months of combat.

delayed_effects.headline: Progressive groups withhold endorsement over perceived moderate SCOTUS pick

choices.text: Nominate a historic first — prioritize diversity and symbolism
choices.outcome: The historic nature of the nomination generated enormous goodwill. Critics attacked the 'identity politics' framing. Confirmation took four months and two votes to spare.

choices.text: Delay the nomination — use the vacancy as political leverage
choices.outcome: The strategy backfired. Both parties attacked you for leaving the court short-staffed. Four major cases were decided 4-4. The press called it a political miscalculation.

title: AI Automation Wave Eliminates 3 Million Jobs
description: A new wave of AI deployment across manufacturing, logistics, and white-collar sectors has eliminated an estimated 3 million jobs in 18 months. Unemployment is ticking up fast. Tech companies are posting record profits simultaneously. The political coalition around this issue cuts across all traditional lines.

choices.text: Propose an AI automation tax to fund displaced worker retraining
choices.outcome: The policy polled 67% approval. Tech lobbying killed it in committee. A watered-down version passed six months later. Workers noticed. Tech CEOs complained.

choices.text: Regulate AI deployment pace through executive order
choices.outcome: Tech companies threatened to move operations offshore. Two relocated R&D to Canada. The regulation slowed domestic AI deployment but the jobs didn't come back.

choices.text: Embrace it — fund massive retraining programs and universal basic income pilot
choices.outcome: The UBI pilot in three states showed promising results. The debt cost alarmed fiscal conservatives. The conversation shifted meaningfully for the first time.

choices.text: Partner with tech industry on voluntary retraining pledges
choices.outcome: Tech companies signed pledges retraining 200,000 workers. Critics noted 3 million were displaced. The 'voluntary partnership' messaging was widely mocked as inadequate.

title: Assassination Attempt on the President
description: A gunman opened fire at a public event. You were unharmed. One Secret Service agent was wounded. The suspect is in custody. The country is in shock. Every decision in the next 48 hours will define how history remembers this moment.

choices.text: Address the nation calmly and refuse to use the event politically
choices.outcome: The address was widely called one of the finest presidential moments in a generation. Approval hit its highest point of the term. The opposition went momentarily silent.

choices.text: Use the moment to push gun control legislation through the emotional wave
choices.outcome: Half the country called it brave. Half called it exploitative. The gun control bill stalled. The political calculation damaged a moment that could have been above politics.

choices.text: Immediately increase security powers and expand federal law enforcement
choices.outcome: Security measures passed rapidly through a shaken Congress. Civil liberties groups filed challenges within weeks. The expanded powers became a permanent fixture.

choices.text: Blame the opposition's rhetoric for creating the climate of violence
choices.outcome: The accusation was called the worst presidential statement in decades. Three cabinet members privately considered resigning. The opposition unified in outrage. A historic miscalculation.

title: China Begins Military Exercises Around Taiwan
description: China has launched the largest military exercises in the Taiwan Strait in 30 years, including live-fire drills within 12 nautical miles of Taiwan's coast. Taiwan is requesting a US carrier strike group deployment. The situation could escalate to conflict within days.

choices.text: Deploy a carrier strike group to the Taiwan Strait as a deterrent
choices.outcome: China backed down within 72 hours. The deterrent worked. The economic relationship with China fractured. Trade disruptions cost American businesses $40B in the quarter.

choices.text: Issue a strong diplomatic statement but take no military action
choices.outcome: China interpreted the response as weakness. Exercises continued for two additional weeks. Taiwan's government publicly expressed concern about American commitment.

choices.text: Rally G7 allies for a coordinated multilateral response
choices.outcome: The multilateral pressure was more effective than unilateral action. China scaled back exercises after ten days. The coalition remained intact for future contingencies.

choices.text: Open backdoor negotiations with Beijing to find a face-saving exit
choices.outcome: The secret talks worked but leaked within a month. You were accused of appeasing China. Taiwan felt abandoned. The resolution was real but the political cost was significant.

title: Bureau of Economic Analysis Confirms Recession
description: Two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth. The R-word is official. Unemployment is at 6.8% and climbing. Mortgage delinquencies are rising. The opposition has been saying this was coming for months and they're already running ads.

choices.text: Announce an emergency economic stimulus package targeting working families
choices.outcome: The stimulus checks arrived quickly. The economy began recovering within two quarters. Debt hawks were furious. Voters who got checks were not.

choices.text: Focus on deficit reduction — cut spending to restore business confidence
choices.outcome: The austerity deepened the recession for two additional quarters. Business confidence didn't respond to spending cuts the way the models predicted. History will not be kind.

choices.text: Invest $3T in green infrastructure to create jobs and future-proof the economy
choices.outcome: The investment created 2.3 million jobs over three years. The upfront cost was politically brutal. The long-term economic data vindicated the bet. History will be kind.

choices.text: Blame the Fed's interest rate policy and pressure them publicly to cut rates
choices.outcome: Fed Chair publicly rejected political pressure. Markets interpreted presidential interference as panic. The dollar weakened. Institutional credibility took a measurable hit.

title: North Korea Tests an ICBM Capable of Reaching the US
description: North Korea successfully tested an intercontinental ballistic missile with estimated range capable of hitting the continental United States. The UN Security Council emergency meeting is underway. Your military advisors are presenting options. South Korea and Japan are on high alert.

choices.text: Coordinate a unified response with South Korea, Japan, and the G7
choices.outcome: The allied coalition response sent a clear message. North Korea paused further testing for four months. The multilateral framework held and was strengthened.

choices.text: Announce expanded missile defense deployment to Japan and South Korea
choices.outcome: The deployment increased regional security significantly. China protested loudly and increased economic pressure on South Korea in retaliation.

choices.text: Open direct backchannel negotiations with Pyongyang
choices.outcome: The talks were secret for three weeks before leaking. The opposition called it naive appeasement. The negotiations produced a temporary testing moratorium.

choices.text: Request a UN Security Council emergency resolution and new sanctions package
choices.outcome: China vetoed the strongest elements. A watered-down sanctions package passed. North Korea ignored it. The UN pathway was exhausted for the near term.

title: Debt Ceiling Standoff — Default in 10 Days
description: The Treasury Department has notified Congress that extraordinary measures to avoid default will be exhausted in 10 days. The opposition is refusing to raise the ceiling without massive spending cuts. A US default would be catastrophic for global markets.

choices.text: Negotiate a bipartisan deal with real spending concessions
choices.outcome: The deal was painful. Both bases complained. Markets breathed. The government avoided default by 4 days. The spending cuts will cost politically for the rest of the term.

choices.text: Invoke the 14th Amendment — argue the debt ceiling is unconstitutional
choices.outcome: Legal scholars were divided. Markets were relieved then nervous again as the legal battle began. The Supreme Court granted cert. The uncertainty lasted eight months.

choices.text: Mint a trillion-dollar platinum coin to sidestep the ceiling entirely
choices.outcome: The move worked technically. The global reaction was somewhere between baffled and alarmed. The dollar weakened 4%. Every future Treasury auction became slightly more expensive.

choices.text: Let it go to the wire — call the opposition's bluff publicly
choices.outcome: Markets collapsed 11% in the final three days. A last-minute deal came together but the damage was done. Moody's downgraded US debt. The global economy took a measurable hit.

title: Mass Shooting at Elementary School
description: A gunman killed 19 children and 2 teachers at an elementary school in Ohio. The nation is in shock. Every parent in America is watching you. The political pressure to act is unlike anything you've faced. So is the opposition from gun rights groups.

choices.text: Push immediate universal background check and red flag law legislation
choices.outcome: The legislation passed with four Republican crossover votes. Gun rights groups launched a primary challenge effort against the Republicans who voted yes. It was ugly but it passed.

delayed_effects.headline: Gun lobby launches $40M campaign targeting vulnerable members who backed gun reform

choices.text: Issue an executive order on ghost guns and assault-style weapon accessories
choices.outcome: The executive action was limited in scope but moved quickly. Legal challenges came within days. The action was upheld 6 months later. A partial win.

choices.text: Convene a bipartisan commission on school safety
choices.outcome: The commission was widely condemned as kicking the can. Parents of victims held press conferences outside the White House. The commission's report came out 11 months later.

choices.text: Focus on mental health funding rather than gun restrictions
choices.outcome: The mental health framing bought bipartisan goodwill but satisfied almost no one on the underlying issue. Grieving families called it a deflection. Funding passed easily.

title: Russia Threatens Nuclear Use in Ukraine Conflict
description: Russian state media and the Defense Ministry have issued explicit nuclear threats in response to recent Ukrainian battlefield gains. Intelligence agencies assess the threat as potentially credible. Your NATO allies are divided on how to respond. The world is holding its breath.

choices.text: Issue clear, credible red line statement with NATO allies jointly
choices.outcome: The joint statement's credibility appeared to work as a deterrent. Russia scaled back the rhetoric within a week. The alliance held and was strengthened.

choices.text: Begin behind-the-scenes negotiations for a Ukraine ceasefire
choices.outcome: Ukraine felt betrayed. European allies were furious at the unilateral approach. Russia interpreted it as weakness. The negotiations produced nothing.

choices.text: Dramatically increase military support to Ukraine to end the war faster
choices.outcome: Ukraine's battlefield position improved significantly. Russia threatened additional escalation. War-weary voters punished you in polling. History may yet vindicate the call.

choices.text: Convene emergency UN Security Council session and demand Russian withdrawal
choices.outcome: Russia vetoed the resolution. The session created international clarity about who the aggressor was. The UN process was exhausted but the diplomatic positioning was solid.

title: Federal Immigration Raids Spark National Controversy
description: A series of federal immigration enforcement raids in sanctuary cities made national headlines after images went viral. Children were separated from parents in two documented cases. Your DHS secretary is defending the operations. Protestors are surrounding ICE facilities in four cities.

choices.text: Pause the raids pending an independent review of procedures
choices.outcome: The pause de-escalated protests immediately. Immigration hardliners were furious. The review took 3 months. Enforcement resumed with new guidelines that satisfied almost no one.

choices.text: Defend the raids and expand them to additional sanctuary cities
choices.outcome: Protests spread to 60 cities. Three governors declared their states sanctuary states by executive order. The federal-state standoff became a constitutional crisis.

choices.text: Fire the DHS officials responsible for the family separations
choices.outcome: Accountability calmed the situation significantly. Immigration enforcement critics called it a genuine gesture. Hardliners called it capitulation. Polls sided with you.

choices.text: Push a comprehensive immigration reform bill to resolve the underlying issue
choices.outcome: The bill became the fourth comprehensive immigration reform attempt to die in Congress. The effort earned credit for trying. The underlying crisis continued.

title: Student Loan Default Wave Hitting the Economy
description: Federal student loan repayments resumed after a multi-year pause. Default rates are running at 22% — far higher than projected. Consumer spending is contracting. A generation of borrowers is locked out of the housing market. The political pressure from your own party is intense.

choices.text: Cancel up to $20,000 in federal student debt via executive action
choices.outcome: The legal challenge reached the Supreme Court within weeks. The Court struck down half the cancellation. What remained helped 14 million borrowers. The political energy was enormous.

choices.text: Income-based repayment reform and interest rate caps only
choices.outcome: The reform was durable and meaningful but lacked the political pop of full cancellation. Borrowers appreciated the relief. Activists demanded more. A solid policy without a headline.

delayed_effects.headline: CBO scores student debt cancellation at $1.1T — borrowing costs rise
Economists warn debt relief is adding inflationary pressure to the economy

choices.text: Full federal student debt cancellation for all borrowers
choices.outcome: The $1.7T price tag was staggering. The legal fight was brutal. The policy reached 43 million people. Future generations will debate whether it was fair or wise.

choices.text: Propose tuition-free community college and vocational training instead
choices.outcome: The forward-looking proposal got bipartisan support. Current borrowers felt left behind. The policy created a better future system without fixing the present crisis.

title: Mega-Fire Threatens Multiple California Cities
description: An unprecedented firestorm driven by historic drought conditions is threatening suburban areas outside Los Angeles. 200,000 residents under evacuation orders. Air quality is dangerous across the Western US. The California governor is requesting an emergency declaration and direct federal assistance.

choices.text: Immediately declare federal emergency and deploy all available resources
choices.outcome: A swift federal response prevented the fire from reaching dense residential areas. 30,000 homes were saved. The cost was significant. The competence was unambiguous.

choices.text: Declare emergency but blame California's forest management policies
choices.outcome: The blame-shifting went over poorly while homes were burning. Assistance arrived slower because of the political noise. The Governor went on national TV to confront you directly.

choices.text: Use the crisis to push landmark climate change legislation
choices.outcome: The crisis created political space for the climate bill. It passed after eight weeks of brutal negotiation. The most significant climate legislation in American history. The timing was uncomfortable.

choices.text: Deploy National Guard and ask neighboring states to share resources
choices.outcome: The mutual aid approach worked but took longer. The cooperation between states was praised as an example of federalism working. Costs were manageable.

title: Whistleblower Claims White House Corruption
description: A former White House official has filed a protected whistleblower complaint alleging that political appointees improperly influenced federal contracts worth $4 billion. The Inspector General is investigating. The story is everywhere.

choices.text: Immediately cooperate fully with IG investigation and release all documents
choices.outcome: Full transparency limited the political damage. The investigation found irregularities but no criminal conduct. The story ended in two weeks rather than two months.

choices.text: Claim executive privilege over all relevant communications
choices.outcome: The privilege claim kept documents hidden but made the story look worse. Congress issued subpoenas. The legal fight lasted nine months. The corruption narrative stuck.

choices.text: Discredit the whistleblower publicly and question their motives
choices.outcome: The attack on the whistleblower united the opposition, alienated moderates, and generated three additional whistleblowers within four weeks. A catastrophic political decision.

choices.text: Fire the officials named in the complaint and announce internal reforms
choices.outcome: Swift accountability and visible reform contained the story. Critics noted the fired officials took the fall for a culture that came from above. The narrative was manageable.

title: Major Bridge Collapses During Rush Hour
description: A major Interstate bridge spanning the Mississippi River collapsed during rush hour. Preliminary reports indicate 34 vehicles were on the bridge. The infrastructure failure exposes decades of deferred maintenance. 45% of US bridges are rated in fair or poor condition.

choices.text: Declare infrastructure emergency and push an immediate repair funding bill
choices.outcome: The bill passed with unexpected bipartisan support. 11,000 bridges were fast-tracked for inspection and repair. The tragedy became the catalyst for something lasting.

choices.text: Launch a full federal investigation into inspection failures
choices.outcome: The investigation found systemic inspection failures across multiple administrations. The report was damning. Accountability came. Repairs remained underfunded.

choices.text: Use existing infrastructure funds and issue emergency repair bonds
choices.outcome: A practical, fiscally conservative approach. Work began immediately on 500 priority bridges. The scope was too small to address the systemic problem but it was tangible.

choices.text: Propose a landmark 10-year national infrastructure modernization plan
choices.outcome: The most ambitious infrastructure proposal in 70 years. The $5T price tag stunned fiscal hawks. The plan took 18 months to pass in modified form. America's infrastructure improved measurably.

title: Major Hospital Systems Announce Closure Across Rural America
description: 24 rural hospital systems serving 4 million Americans have announced closure within 90 days citing unsustainable operating losses. Rural healthcare deserts are expanding. 85,000 healthcare workers face unemployment. The political implications cut across every demographic.

choices.text: Emergency federal operating subsidies to keep rural hospitals open
choices.outcome: The hospitals stayed open. The subsidies became permanent line items. Rural voters rewarded you in every subsequent poll. Fiscal hawks hated it. Nurses loved it.

choices.text: Push public option expansion to cover the rural healthcare gap
choices.outcome: The public option passed after six weeks of brutal negotiation. Insurance industry launched a $400M opposition campaign. Rural access improved measurably within a year.

choices.text: Create telehealth expansion program and rural medical school incentives
choices.outcome: A targeted, bipartisan approach. Not enough to save the closing hospitals but meaningful for long-term rural health access. The hospitals still closed. The future improved.

choices.text: Let market forces handle it — announce deregulation to attract private capital
choices.outcome: Private capital didn't arrive. Rural counties lost their only hospital. Maternal mortality spiked in six states. The political fallout lasted the rest of the term.

title: Intelligence Report: Foreign Election Interference Underway
description: A classified intelligence assessment confirms a foreign government is actively running a sophisticated social media influence operation targeting the upcoming midterm elections. The operation is effective and accelerating. Public disclosure could cause panic. Silence makes you complicit.

choices.text: Declassify and publicly attribute the operation — let voters decide
choices.outcome: Public attribution created chaos but also awareness. Voters were disturbed but not panicked. The foreign operation continued but with reduced effectiveness.

choices.text: Covert cyber operation to disrupt the foreign interference network
choices.outcome: The covert operation disrupted the network for six weeks. It restarted from new infrastructure. The public never knew. The security gain was real and temporary.

choices.text: Brief Congressional leadership privately and coordinate a joint response
choices.outcome: Bipartisan congressional coordination produced a strong, unified response. The operation was partially disrupted. The process worked as designed.

choices.text: Impose additional sanctions and expel diplomatic personnel
choices.outcome: The diplomatic expulsion sent a clear signal. The foreign government responded in kind, expelling US diplomats. The intelligence operation continued from third-country infrastructure.

title: Federal Reserve Rate Decision Rattles Markets
description: The Fed just announced a surprise 0.75% rate hike to combat persistent inflation. Mortgage rates hit 8.5%. The housing market froze overnight. Your economic advisors are split on whether to publicly pressure the Fed or stay silent about an independent institution.

choices.text: Publicly back the Fed and defend its independence
choices.outcome: Markets appreciated the institutional discipline. The political press called it cowardly. Homebuyers were furious. The long-term economic credibility was preserved.

choices.text: Publicly pressure the Fed to reverse course immediately
choices.outcome: Markets interpreted presidential pressure on the Fed as panic. The dollar weakened. The Fed Chair publicly rejected political interference. Your credibility took a lasting hit.

choices.text: Announce targeted relief for first-time homebuyers to offset rate impact
choices.outcome: The relief program addressed the immediate political pain without touching Fed independence. A surgical response that satisfied most stakeholders. Fiscally modest.

choices.text: Call for congressional hearings on Federal Reserve accountability
choices.outcome: The hearing threat unnerved markets. The dollar weakened. The hearings produced nothing actionable. The Fed continued as designed. The optics were poor.

title: Midterm Election Results Reshape Your Presidency
description: Midterm results are in. The outcome depends on how you've governed. Your legislative path for the second half of your term is about to be determined. This is the pivot point of your presidency.

choices.text: Claim the results as a mandate and push an ambitious second-half agenda
choices.outcome: The bold framing energized your base. The agenda had mixed success. But the narrative gave your second half political momentum that the opposition struggled to contain.

choices.text: Pivot to the center — announce bipartisan priorities for the second half
choices.outcome: The pivot paid off in Congress but cost you with your base. The bipartisan bills that passed were meaningful. The base started talking about a primary challenger.

choices.text: Use executive orders to bypass a hostile Congress
choices.outcome: The executive actions moved policy but poisoned the congressional relationship for 24 months. Legal challenges ate most of the wins. The opposition rallied around judicial appointments.

choices.text: Accept the results gracefully and focus on areas of genuine bipartisan overlap
choices.outcome: The graceful response generated goodwill. Bipartisan bills on veterans, infrastructure, and fentanyl passed. Your base thought you gave too much away. History was more generous.

title: A Prominent Figure in Your Party Announces Primary Challenge
description: A popular governor from within your own party has announced a primary challenge to your reelection bid, citing your 'betrayal of core principles.' Internal polls show them at 28% in early primary states. Your base is shaken.

choices.text: Ignore the challenger and focus on governing — don't dignify it
choices.outcome: The non-response looked confident to some, detached to others. The challenger gained 6 more points in the polls before peaking. They never broke through.

choices.text: Move sharply back toward your base on signature issues
choices.outcome: The base consolidation worked. The challenger faded. Independent voters noticed the lurch and moved away. A successful defense that cost you in the general.

choices.text: Bring the challenger into the administration with a senior appointment
choices.outcome: The rival accepted. The primary threat evaporated. The appointment brought real talent. There was also now someone with ambitions inside your tent.

choices.text: Use party machinery to freeze the challenger out of resources and ballot access
choices.outcome: The challenger cried foul publicly. Press coverage made you look like you feared them. The challenge ended but the optics cost more than the threat.

title: Major Cartel Offensive Kills 12 Americans in Mexico
description: A cartel ambush in northern Mexico killed 12 American tourists including three children. The Mexican government's response was inadequate. Senators from both parties are calling for US military intervention. The public wants action.

choices.text: Deploy US special forces for targeted cartel leadership elimination
choices.outcome: Four cartel leaders were killed in 30 days. Mexico's government formally protested the sovereignty violation. The cartel splintered into smaller, harder-to-target cells.

choices.text: Demand Mexico act or face sanctions and border restrictions
choices.outcome: Mexico ramped up enforcement under pressure. The cartel offensive paused. The bilateral relationship took a significant hit that cost both economies.

choices.text: Increase intelligence sharing and DEA operations with Mexican cooperation
choices.outcome: The cooperative approach produced real results over six months. Critics called it too slow. The cartel was degraded without a sovereignty crisis.

choices.text: Formally designate cartels as foreign terrorist organizations
choices.outcome: The FTO designation unlocked new legal tools and froze cartel assets. Mexico called it an insult to their sovereignty. Congress cheered. The cartels adapted.

title: Big Tech CEOs Testify — Congress Wants Regulation
description: A Senate hearing into social media's role in teen mental health crisis, political polarization, and election misinformation went viral. Public pressure for tech regulation is at an all-time high. The tech industry is lobbying hard against any action. Your FTC chair is ready to move.

choices.text: Push comprehensive social media regulation and antitrust enforcement
choices.outcome: The legislation passed. Tech stocks dropped 15%. Teen screen time metrics improved within a year. Tech companies began shifting operations to more permissive jurisdictions.

choices.text: Order FTC to pursue Section 2 antitrust action against the largest platforms
choices.outcome: The antitrust cases moved slowly through courts. Platform behavior was modified marginally. The legal pressure was real but results came years later.

choices.text: Pursue voluntary industry agreements on content moderation
choices.outcome: Tech companies signed pledges and then largely ignored them. Congress called the voluntary approach a sham. The press was brutal. Public trust in the tech-government relationship hit a new low.

choices.text: Create a new independent Digital Safety Agency with real enforcement power
choices.outcome: The new agency took 18 months to stand up. The structure was copied by EU and UK regulators. A lasting institutional contribution to how democracies handle platform power.

title: Fentanyl Overdose Deaths Hit Record High
description: Annual overdose deaths crossed 120,000 for the first time — more Americans than die in car accidents. Chinese precursor chemicals flowing through Mexican cartels are the primary supply chain. Every community in America is affected. The political coalition to address this crosses party lines.

choices.text: Treat it as a public health crisis — massive investment in treatment and harm reduction
choices.outcome: Overdose deaths dropped 18% over two years. Critics called the approach permissive. Public health experts called it the right thing. Deaths fell and that mattered.

choices.text: Treat it as a national security threat — military-backed supply chain disruption
choices.outcome: Enforcement disrupted major supply networks. New networks emerged within months. Deaths dropped 8% before stabilizing. The disruption bought time without solving the problem.

choices.text: Coordinate with China to shut down precursor chemical exports
choices.outcome: A diplomatic coup. China agreed to schedule 13 precursor chemicals. Fentanyl supply dropped measurably within a year. The cooperation came with trade concessions.

choices.text: Federal death penalty for fentanyl trafficking convictions
choices.outcome: The policy energized law-and-order voters. Civil liberties groups launched a major opposition campaign. Deaths continued at record rates. Deterrence effects on trafficking were negligible.

title: Housing Affordability Hits All-Time Low
description: The median American family can now afford only 12% of homes on the market — the lowest affordability ratio since records began. Homelessness is rising in every major city. Young voters are furious. The political coalition on housing policy is scrambled along new lines.

choices.text: Federal land release and zoning reform to dramatically increase housing supply
choices.outcome: The supply shock took 3 years to materialize but was real. Homebuilders loved it. Existing homeowners in desirable markets were not pleased. Long-term correct call.

choices.text: National rent control framework for housing emergencies
choices.outcome: Renters cheered. Landlords pulled 200,000 units from the market within a year. Supply contracted. The problem worsened in the medium term despite short-term relief.

choices.text: First-time homebuyer down payment assistance and mortgage relief program
choices.outcome: 2 million families got meaningful help. The program was oversubscribed in 11 days. It helped buyers at the margins without addressing the underlying supply deficit.

choices.text: Massive federal investment in public and affordable housing construction
choices.outcome: 500,000 affordable units were built over four years. The most significant public housing investment since the New Deal. The cost was immense. The impact on homelessness was measurable.

title: VA Hospitals Falsifying Wait Times — Veterans Dying
description: An investigation reveals VA hospitals across nine states have been falsifying wait time data. Dozens of veterans died waiting for care that never came. Veterans groups are furious. Congress is demanding hearings. This cuts across every political line.

choices.text: Fire the VA Secretary immediately and announce a complete system overhaul
choices.outcome: Swift accountability and a genuine overhaul plan generated bipartisan praise. The reform process was painful but real. Veterans began receiving measurably better care within 18 months.

choices.text: Expand the Veterans Choice Program to allow private healthcare options
choices.outcome: Veterans gained real access to private care quickly. Critics warned about VA system atrophy. The Choice program was widely used and broadly popular with veterans themselves.

choices.text: Convene a commission and promise accountability within 90 days
choices.outcome: Veterans groups called the commission 'another delay.' Two senators from your party went on television to condemn the response. The commission's 90-day report changed nothing.

choices.text: Fully fund VA expansion, hire 15,000 new staff, and build 40 new facilities
choices.outcome: The most significant investment in veteran healthcare since the GI Bill. Wait times dropped 60% within two years. The debt cost was real. Veterans never forgot who fought for them.

title: Supreme Court Issues Landmark Abortion Ruling
description: The Supreme Court has issued a 5-4 ruling with major implications for abortion access across the country. The decision will affect 40 million women of reproductive age. Protests are already forming on both sides. Your response in the next 24 hours will define your legacy on this issue.

choices.text: Immediately propose federal legislation to codify abortion rights into law
choices.outcome: The bill galvanized your base and infuriated the opposition. It failed in the Senate by four votes. The fight defined the political landscape for the rest of the term.

choices.text: Issue executive orders protecting access to medication abortion and interstate travel
choices.outcome: The executive actions protected access for millions immediately. Legal challenges came within hours. Half the actions were upheld. The practical impact was significant.

choices.text: Respect the Court's ruling and leave it to states to decide
choices.outcome: The response destroyed your relationship with a core constituency. Primary polling shifted 12 points against you within a week. The decision haunted every subsequent campaign event.

choices.text: Propose adding justices to the Court to change the majority
choices.outcome: The court-packing proposal became the story, not the ruling. Democratic senators refused to support it. The institutional damage framing dominated. The underlying issue got less airtime.

title: Administration Leaks Trigger Journalist Prosecution
description: Your DOJ has charged a journalist with receiving classified information — a significant escalation of press freedom tensions. The news media is in open revolt. The First Amendment Foundation and 200 press organizations have signed a joint letter. Congress is alarmed.

choices.text: Order DOJ to drop the charges and reaffirm press freedom principles
choices.outcome: The decision generated a wave of press goodwill. Your media sentiment score improved measurably. Intelligence agencies complained about the chilling effect on leak investigations.

choices.text: Let the prosecution proceed — no one is above the law
choices.outcome: Press coverage became uniformly hostile. Every subsequent scandal was covered with maximum aggression. The prosecution eventually failed in court after 14 months.

choices.text: Propose a federal shield law protecting journalists from prosecution
choices.outcome: The shield law passed with bipartisan support. The prosecution was dropped. Your relationship with the press corps improved measurably for the rest of the term.

choices.text: Publicly criticize the media while technically allowing the case to continue
choices.outcome: The split approach satisfied no one. The prosecution continued. The media war intensified. Your base approved of the combative stance. No one else did.

title: Adversary Destroys Satellite — US Assets at Risk
description: China has tested an anti-satellite weapon by destroying one of its own satellites in low-Earth orbit. The debris field is threatening US military and commercial satellites. It's widely seen as a direct warning shot. Your Space Force commander is requesting authorization to respond.

choices.text: Announce the US will treat attacks on satellites as acts of war going forward
choices.outcome: The doctrine statement created clarity and credibility. China protested. No further satellite tests occurred for 18 months. The commercial space industry applauded the protection.

choices.text: Accelerate hardening of US satellites and redundant systems — no public statement
choices.outcome: The quiet capability investment was more valuable than any diplomatic signal. US satellite infrastructure became significantly more resilient. China noticed but couldn't complain.

choices.text: Propose an international treaty banning anti-satellite weapons
choices.outcome: The treaty proposal was praised internationally. Russia and China refused to negotiate. The effort established moral authority without producing an agreement.

choices.text: Conduct a US counter-demonstration by hardening a military satellite publicly
choices.outcome: The demonstration sent a credible deterrent signal. The arms race in space accelerated. The Pentagon called it a necessary investment. Diplomats called it an escalation.

title: China Announces Sweeping Tariffs on US Goods
description: China retaliated against existing US tariffs with 45% tariffs on American agricultural exports. Soybean and pork prices are collapsing. Midwestern farming communities are facing an existential crisis. The trade war is accelerating. Both sides are digging in.

choices.text: Escalate — add additional tariffs on Chinese electronics and manufactured goods
choices.outcome: Consumer electronics prices rose 12%. China doubled down. The trade war entered a new phase. Manufacturing reshoring accelerated. The short-term pain was real.

choices.text: Negotiate directly with Beijing — offer tariff rollbacks for agricultural access
choices.outcome: The deal was smaller than advertised but real. Soybean prices stabilized. Critics called it capitulation. Farmers in Iowa called it relief. The stock market rallied 4%.

choices.text: Subsidize affected American farmers while building alternative export markets
choices.outcome: The farm subsidies kept rural communities afloat. New trade agreements with India and Vietnam partially replaced lost Chinese demand. A durable if expensive response.

choices.text: Rally an international anti-China trade coalition through the WTO
choices.outcome: The coalition built slowly and held. WTO dispute resolution backed the US position 14 months later. The multilateral approach paid off in ways unilateral action couldn't.

title: Coordinated Attack on Power Grid Leaves Millions Without Power
description: A coordinated physical and cyber attack on power substations has knocked out electricity to 8 million people across the Southeast. Winter temperatures are dropping. Hospitals are on backup power. The attack shows signs of sophisticated planning — possibly state-sponsored.

choices.text: Declare national emergency and mobilize military and utility resources immediately
choices.outcome: Power restored to 80% of affected areas within 72 hours. The military logistics response was praised as exemplary. The vulnerability that was exploited remained for years.

choices.text: Publicly attribute the attack and announce retaliatory cyber operations
choices.outcome: The attribution was correct. The cyber retaliation disrupted the attacker's infrastructure for 60 days. The public response was confident. The escalation risk was managed.

choices.text: Propose mandatory grid hardening legislation with federal investment
choices.outcome: The legislation passed with overwhelming bipartisan support. Grid hardening investment transformed US energy infrastructure over four years. The attack was the last of its kind.

choices.text: Work with utilities privately to restore power without attributing the attack
choices.outcome: Power was restored on the utility's timeline. The quiet approach was criticized as weak. The lack of attribution was later revealed in a leak. The response became a political liability.

title: Legal Immigration System Grinds to a Halt
description: The backlog of legal immigration applications has reached 10 million cases. Skilled workers on H-1B visas are being deported after waiting years. Tech companies are losing talent to Canada and Europe. The dysfunctional legal system is hurting American competitiveness.

choices.text: Emergency USCIS funding to hire 5,000 additional adjudicators
choices.outcome: The backlog began clearing within a year. Tech companies started repatriating talent. Bipartisan support was broad. A quiet competence win that didn't make headlines.

choices.text: Expand high-skilled immigration pathways and increase H-1B caps
choices.outcome: Economic productivity improved measurably. Labor groups complained about competition for jobs. The anti-immigration coalition mobilized. The economic data was unambiguous.

choices.text: Comprehensive immigration reform tying legal immigration to border security
choices.outcome: The comprehensive approach died in Senate again. Pieces of it passed separately over 18 months. Progress was real but slower than the crisis demanded.

choices.text: Issue executive action streamlining the application process
choices.outcome: The executive streamlining worked faster than legislative reform. Legal challenges slowed implementation. The backlog dropped 30% in 18 months before being enjoined by a federal court.

title: Second Largest US Bank on the Verge of Collapse
description: The nation's second-largest commercial bank has seen a catastrophic 60% drop in its stock price amid a bank run triggered by deposit flight. The FDIC is preparing for the largest bank failure in US history. Contagion to other regional banks has begun.

choices.text: FDIC takeover and government backstop of all depositors including uninsured
choices.outcome: The bank run stopped. Contagion was prevented. Critics howled about moral hazard. The systemic risk was real and the intervention was correct. The economy stabilized.

choices.text: Let the bank fail — FDIC only covers $250K per depositor as designed
choices.outcome: Four more banks collapsed within a week. The contagion was severe. Small businesses couldn't make payroll. The 2008 comparisons became unavoidable.

choices.text: Orchestrate private sector rescue — pressure other banks to acquire the failing one
choices.outcome: A private sector solution was assembled in 72 hours. Taxpayer exposure was minimal. The banking system remained stable. The deal required significant regulatory concessions.

choices.text: Emergency temporary nationalization with plan to reprivatize within 2 years
choices.outcome: The nationalization worked technically. The bank was stabilized and sold within 18 months at a modest profit. The political optics of a government-owned bank were challenging throughout.

title: International Climate Summit — US Leadership Moment
description: A major international climate summit is convening in Geneva. 190 countries are watching to see what commitments the US will make. Your domestic climate record is mixed. This is a moment to either lead or follow on the defining issue of the generation.

choices.text: Announce aggressive US emissions targets and $500B in clean energy investment
choices.outcome: The commitment restored US credibility as a climate leader. Allies matched or exceeded the pledges. The domestic investment created 800,000 jobs. History will remember this well.

choices.text: Sign the agreement but set moderate targets — prioritize economic realism
choices.outcome: A credible but unexciting commitment. Allies were underwhelmed. Climate groups were frustrated. The targets were achievable and the economy was protected.

delayed_effects.headline: Allies begin reconsidering trade terms after US climate withdrawal

choices.text: Refuse to sign without enforceable commitments from China and India
choices.outcome: The refusal was framed as principled. Allies were furious. Climate activists organized against you. China and India used the US withdrawal to justify their own inaction.

choices.text: Lead a technology transfer initiative to accelerate developing world clean energy
choices.outcome: The technology transfer approach was innovative and broadly praised. It helped countries that mattered. The commitment of clean energy IP created American soft power dividends for years.

title: Major Opioid Manufacturer Offered $40B Settlement
description: The largest opioid manufacturer in the country has offered a $40 billion settlement over its role in the opioid crisis that killed 500,000 Americans. The settlement would go through bankruptcy, shielding executives from personal liability. Victims' families are furious at the terms.

choices.text: Reject the settlement — DOJ pursues criminal charges against executives
choices.outcome: Criminal charges against six executives proceeded. Three were convicted. The victims' families called it justice. The $40B settlement vanished as assets were tied up in litigation.

delayed_effects.headline: DOJ prosecution costs balloon as pharma defendants mount legal challenges

choices.text: Accept the settlement — $40B for treatment programs is transformative
choices.outcome: The settlement funded treatment for 2 million people. Victims' families felt the executives escaped accountability. The tension between justice and funding was real and unresolved.

delayed_effects.headline: Families of overdose victims call settlement a betrayal — no one goes to jail

choices.text: Counter-offer — $60B settlement with some executive accountability
choices.outcome: The company accepted a modified $52B deal with executive fines but no criminal liability. A compromise that neither fully satisfied justice advocates nor fully protected executives.

choices.text: Use settlement negotiations to pass federal opioid reform legislation
choices.outcome: The reform legislation passed with the settlement as political leverage. Prescribing regulations were tightened. The settlement was modified. A systemic win alongside the specific case.

title: US Embassy Attacked — Americans Killed
description: A coordinated attack on the US Embassy in a major Middle Eastern capital has killed 6 Americans including the Deputy Ambassador. The group claiming responsibility has ties to a regional power you've been trying to negotiate with. The comparison to Benghazi is immediate and relentless.

choices.text: Ordered targeted military strike on the responsible group's leadership
choices.outcome: The strike killed three senior leaders. The regional power denied involvement publicly while privately signaling de-escalation. Congress supported the action 78-22.

choices.text: Hold the sponsoring regional power directly responsible and impose sanctions
choices.outcome: The public attribution ended the diplomatic track you'd been building. The sanctions bit. The regional power shifted toward China. The Benghazi comparisons died.

choices.text: Investigate thoroughly before responding — refuse to be rushed
choices.outcome: The patience was politically devastating. The Benghazi comparisons intensified daily. The investigation confirmed the responsible party 40 days later. The delay cost more than it gained.

choices.text: Immediately close the embassy and evacuate all US personnel from the region
choices.outcome: The evacuation protected American lives but projected weakness across the region. Allies questioned US commitment to the area. Adversaries took note. Rebuilding presence took two years.

title: Opposition Candidate Launches Aggressive Campaign
description: Your opponent in the upcoming election has launched a campaign centered entirely on your economic record and approval rating. Early polls have the race within 3 points. Your campaign team says you need a defining moment to create separation before the final stretch.

choices.text: Schedule a major policy announcement — go big and dominate the news cycle
choices.outcome: The announcement shifted the media conversation for two weeks. The opposition pivoted to attacking the policy. The poll gap opened to 7 points. Momentum shifted.

choices.text: Challenge the opponent to multiple debates — confidence play
choices.outcome: The debate challenge was accepted. You won the first debate on substance. The second was a draw. The third debate moved no numbers. A net positive.

choices.text: Stay presidential — govern, don't campaign. Let your record speak.
choices.outcome: The dignity play polled well with independents. Your party's base wanted more fight. The race tightened to 1 point before a late break your direction.

choices.text: Go aggressively negative — attack the opponent's record and character
choices.outcome: The attacks energized your base but disgusted moderates. The race became a turnout election. Your ability to win depended entirely on base enthusiasm versus moderate disgust.

title: Secretary of State Resigns in Public Disagreement
description: Your Secretary of State has resigned and immediately given a press conference criticizing your foreign policy as 'reckless and uninformed.' The resignation is front-page everywhere. Three foreign leaders have called your office asking for clarity. Your foreign policy team is in chaos.

choices.text: Accept the resignation gracefully and quickly announce a strong replacement
choices.outcome: The quick pivot to a respected replacement reassured allies. The story lasted a week instead of a month. Your foreign policy continued without a lasting gap.

choices.text: Publicly attack the former Secretary's competence and loyalties
choices.outcome: The public fight lasted three weeks. Three more cabinet members signaled dissatisfaction privately. The international community watched with alarm. The story was catastrophically extended.

choices.text: Invite them back for a private conversation and try to resolve the dispute
choices.outcome: The reconciliation talks failed but the attempt looked mature and presidential. The former Secretary stopped giving interviews. A dignified but inconclusive ending.

choices.text: Use it as an opportunity to restructure and strengthen foreign policy leadership
choices.outcome: The restructure produced a stronger team with clearer lines of authority. The press focused on the drama for a week then moved on to the new lineup. An organizational win from a crisis.

title: NSA Program Collecting Data on American Citizens Exposed
description: A leak has revealed a classified NSA program collecting metadata on American citizens' phone calls and internet activity without individual warrants. Civil liberties advocates are outraged. Security hawks say the program is essential. The leak itself is a major intelligence failure.

choices.text: Defend the program as legal and essential to national security
choices.outcome: The defense was legally accurate but politically devastating. Civil liberties groups launched the largest legal campaign against surveillance since the Church Committee. The controversy lasted years.

choices.text: Announce immediate program reform with stronger judicial oversight
choices.outcome: The reform was genuine and meaningful. Civil libertarians acknowledged the progress. Security agencies adapted and found new authorities. A difficult but durable resolution.

choices.text: Shut down the program entirely and prosecute the officials who authorized it
choices.outcome: Civil libertarians celebrated. Intelligence agencies said the loss of the program created genuine security gaps. Two threat assessments in the following year cited the reduced visibility.

choices.text: Focus on prosecuting the leaker and don't address the program directly
choices.outcome: The leaker was arrested but turned the prosecution into a media spectacle. The program remained under a cloud. Press freedom organizations mobilized globally. An ongoing disaster.

title: Iran Announces Enrichment Threshold Crossed
description: Iran has announced it has enriched uranium to 90% purity — weapons-grade. The announcement is a direct challenge to the international non-proliferation order. Israel has signaled it is prepared to act militarily within 30 days if the US does not. Every ally is calling.

choices.text: Coordinate with Israel and allies on joint military options
choices.outcome: The joint operation set Iran's program back 3-5 years. Hezbollah launched retaliatory attacks in three countries. Oil prices spiked 35%. A tense but ultimately contained escalation.

choices.text: Offer comprehensive sanctions relief in exchange for a verifiable rollback
choices.outcome: Iran accepted a deal 90 days later. Verification was contentious. The enriched material was removed to Russia. A fragile but real arrangement that bought 5 years of non-proliferation.

choices.text: Escalate sanctions to maximum pressure and blockade
choices.outcome: Maximum pressure produced no diplomatic breakthrough. Iran accelerated its program in defiance. Oil markets tightened. European allies broke with US policy. The pressure held but produced nothing.

choices.text: Refer the situation to the UN Security Council and build international consensus
choices.outcome: Russia and China blocked the strongest measures. A limited resolution passed. International inspectors were given expanded access. The program remained in place but slowed.

title: Major City's Water System Fails — Lead Contamination
description: Flint-scale lead contamination has been discovered in the water system of a major American city, affecting 800,000 residents. The governor knew for months and didn't act. The city is overwhelmingly low-income. The federal government's jurisdiction is limited but the political pressure is total.

choices.text: Declare federal environmental emergency and fund complete pipe replacement
choices.outcome: Bottled water, filters, and federal oversight arrived within days. The complete pipe replacement took 3 years. Children's health data improved. The response was unambiguously right.

choices.text: Hold the governor accountable — demand state action before federal support
choices.outcome: The jurisdictional purity looked like people suffering while officials argued about responsibility. The governor eventually acted. The timeline cost every child in the city measurable harm.

choices.text: EPA emergency order plus targeted federal funding for immediate relief
choices.outcome: A practical, limited intervention that addressed the immediate crisis without creating a full federal takeover. The long-term pipe replacement remained the state's responsibility.

choices.text: Propose national water infrastructure safety standards and federal oversight
choices.outcome: The systemic reform was right. The 800,000 people waited longer for help while legislation moved. The standards passed and will protect tens of millions in future decades.

title: 150,000 Refugees Fleeing Conflict Request Entry
description: A humanitarian catastrophe in a war-torn region has produced 150,000 refugees requesting asylum or refugee status. International allies are requesting the US accept a proportionate share. The political coalition on refugee policy is volatile and intensely watched.

choices.text: Accept 50,000 refugees through expedited vetting process
choices.outcome: The admission was praised internationally and by human rights organizations. Anti-immigration groups launched a major political campaign. Resettlement was largely successful.

choices.text: Refuse refugee admission — prioritize national security concerns
choices.outcome: International standing took a significant hit. Allies reduced intelligence sharing in protest. Domestic human rights organizations mobilized against you. Security improved marginally.

choices.text: Accept 10,000 with extremely rigorous vetting — slower but thorough
choices.outcome: The cautious approach satisfied security hawks and some moderates. It was too little for allies and human rights groups. A politically safe choice with real humanitarian costs.

choices.text: Increase foreign aid dramatically to address the crisis at its source
choices.outcome: The upstream approach was praised by development economists. Refugees continued to arrive while the aid worked its way through systems. A correct long-term bet with short-term suffering.

title: Sanctions Regime Causing Humanitarian Crisis Abroad
description: US economic sanctions on a hostile nation are causing a documented civilian humanitarian crisis — food and medicine shortages affecting 4 million people. International allies are calling for modifications. The sanctioned government is using the crisis to deflect blame for its own failures.

choices.text: Maintain sanctions but carve out humanitarian exemptions for food and medicine
choices.outcome: The humanitarian carve-outs reduced civilian suffering while maintaining economic pressure. The sanctioned government exploited the optics anyway. Allies were satisfied.

choices.text: Lift all sanctions — the civilian cost has become unjustifiable
choices.outcome: The sanctioned government declared victory. The diplomatic pressure evaporated. The humanitarian situation improved. Security analysts called it a strategic retreat.

choices.text: Maintain and intensify sanctions — don't let the government use civilians as shields
choices.outcome: The intensification isolated the US from allies who broke from the sanctions coalition. The pressure worked economically but the international isolation cost more.

choices.text: Use the humanitarian crisis as leverage to negotiate a diplomatic settlement
choices.outcome: The diplomatic opening produced a partial agreement within three months. The crisis eased. The settlement was imperfect but real. An exit from a bad situation on reasonable terms.

title: Inflation Hits 9% — Highest in 40 Years
description: Consumer price inflation has hit 9.1% annually. Grocery bills are up 14%. Rent up 22%. The political pain is acute — this is the most tangible economic problem in a generation. Your economic team is divided and there are no painless options.

choices.text: Support aggressive Fed rate hikes and accept short-term economic pain
choices.outcome: The medicine was bitter. Inflation peaked and began falling within two quarters. Unemployment rose to 5.8% during the adjustment. The approach was correct. The short-term pain was real.

choices.text: Price controls on essential goods — groceries, gas, and rent
choices.outcome: The controls were popular in the short term. Economists were unanimous in their horror. Shortages developed within three months. Black markets emerged. The policy was an economic disaster.

choices.text: Targeted relief checks to low and middle income households
choices.outcome: The checks provided meaningful relief to struggling families. Economists noted it added demand at exactly the wrong time, slightly extending the inflationary period. A humane choice with a cost.

choices.text: Massive domestic energy production expansion to reduce costs
choices.outcome: The production expansion took 18 months to materialize. Energy prices did fall significantly. Climate groups were furious. The long-term inflation benefit was real but slow.

title: Historic Peace Deal Within Reach — Two Ancient Rivals
description: Secret negotiations you've been quietly hosting between two historically hostile nations have produced a framework for normalization of relations. The deal would be the most significant diplomatic achievement in a generation. But finalizing it requires political capital and real concessions from the US.

choices.text: Commit fully — host the signing ceremony at the White House
choices.outcome: The signing was the most-watched diplomatic event in 25 years. Your legacy was transformed in a single afternoon. The deal held for 18 months before being tested. It survived.

choices.text: Support the deal but let the parties take credit
choices.outcome: The deal was announced. The parties got the credit. The durability was better for the low US profile. Your role was known to those who mattered. Historians documented it fully.

choices.text: Demand more concessions before committing US support
choices.outcome: One party walked away. The deal collapsed. It took three years to reassemble the diplomatic conditions. You spent the rest of your term knowing you had it and let it go.

choices.text: Leak the negotiations to generate domestic political momentum
choices.outcome: Both parties felt betrayed by the leak. The negotiations collapsed. The diplomatic community's trust in US discretion was damaged. A historic opportunity was lost for domestic political gain.

title: Supreme Court Strikes Down Your Gun Law
description: The Supreme Court has ruled 5-4 that your signature gun safety legislation exceeds federal constitutional authority. The ruling strikes down universal background checks and assault weapon restrictions. The opposition is celebrating. Your base is furious. You have a decision to make.

choices.text: Announce a constitutional amendment campaign — take it to the people
choices.outcome: The amendment campaign galvanized your base but failed to reach the 2/3 threshold. The effort revealed exactly which senators were movable. A loss that built future political architecture.

choices.text: Work within the ruling to pass narrower but constitutional gun measures
choices.outcome: Three targeted measures passed within the Court's new limits. Incremental progress was real. Activists called it surrender. The practical effect was meaningful.

choices.text: Accept the ruling — respect the Court's constitutional authority
choices.outcome: The judicial restraint approach was constitutionally defensible and politically catastrophic. Your base never forgave you for not fighting. The issue defined your legacy negatively.

choices.text: Issue executive orders using existing statutory authority to preserve what you can
choices.outcome: The executive actions preserved meaningful elements of the policy within the Court's ruling. Legal challenges to the orders were largely unsuccessful. A partial win from a significant loss.

title: Street Protests Turn Violent in 15 Cities
description: Weeks of accumulated protests over economic inequality and police violence have turned violent in 15 major cities. Property damage is extensive. Three protesters have been killed in confrontations. Your response will either contain or accelerate the spiral.

choices.text: National address calling for peace while announcing concrete policy reforms
choices.outcome: The address hit the right notes. The combination of accountability and de-escalation language worked. Violence wound down within a week. The underlying tensions required sustained attention.

choices.text: Deploy federal law enforcement to restore order — zero tolerance
choices.outcome: The federal presence inflamed the situation. Cities that had been calm erupted. The images went global. The crackdown became the story and extended the crisis by three weeks.

choices.text: Give state and local authorities 48 hours to restore order before federal action
choices.outcome: Most cities restored order on their own. Two required National Guard deployment. The measured federal stance preserved both order and institutional relationships.

choices.text: Announce emergency economic assistance for the communities most affected
